diagrams.title: Four Conditions of Deadlock
diagrams.description: Venn diagram showing four overlapping circles representing the four necessary conditions. All four must overlap (intersect) for deadlock to occur in the center.
elements: Prevention region (removing any one circle breaks deadlock)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding that all four conditions are necessary and preventing any one breaks deadlock

diagrams.title: Resource Ordering for Circular Wait Prevention
diagrams.description: Staircase diagram showing resource types arranged in increasing order (A=1, B=2, C=3, D=4). A process can only move UP the staircase (request higher-numbered resources).
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how resource ordering prevents circular wait by creating a partial ordering

diagrams.title: Hold and Wait Prevention Methods
diagrams.description: Two contrasting flowcharts showing (a) request all resources upfront - wait until all available, and (b) release-then-request - release held resources before requesting new ones.
diagrams.purpose: Understanding two different approaches to prevent Hold and Wait condition and their trade-offs
